test_cases.title: Verify that a user can successfully log time with valid inputs.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an active Project from Brac/BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for Hours.
expected_result: The entry should be successfully created and displayed in the Timesheet Management module.
The system should confirm successful submission.

test_cases.title: Ensure that invalid or missing inputs block submission.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
5. Leave the Work Description field blank.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that Hours and Work Description are mandatory fields.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Validate that Hours must be numeric and greater than zero.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an active Project from Brac/BracIT Jira.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that Hours must be greater than zero.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Confirm that the system allows submitting completed entries.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an active Project from Brac/BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for Hours.
expected_result: The entry should be successfully marked as completed and displayed in the Timesheet Management module.
The system should confirm successful completion.

test_cases.title: Verify that the system does not allow logging without allocation.
preconditions: User is NOT allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an active Project from Brac/BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for Hours.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the user must be allocated to the project.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Validate that the list of Work Log Categories is static and taken from the portfolio manager.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Verify that the available Work Log Categories match those listed in the portfolio manager.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for Hours.
expected_result: The system should display the correct Work Log Categories.
The entry should be successfully created.

test_cases.title: Confirm that Project is searchable from both Brac and BracIT Jira and shown list is assigned to the user.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Verify that the Project list is populated with projects from Brac and BracIT Jira.
5. Select a project from the list.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for Hours.
expected_result: The Project list should display projects from both Brac and BracIT Jira.
The entry should be successfully created.

test_cases.title: Verify that the date selection allows only dates from the current month.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Attempt to select a date from the previous month.
4. Attempt to select a date from the next month.
expected_result: The date picker should only allow selection of dates from the current month.
The system should display an error message or prevent selection of dates outside the current month.

test_cases.title: Assert that data fields validation prevents invalid data entry.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that Hours must be a numeric value.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Validate that the system handles archived projects correctly.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select an archived Project from the list.
5. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for Hours.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the project is archived and cannot be selected.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Verify that the system allows entry with zero hours.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an active Project from Brac/BracIT Jira.
expected_result: The entry should be saved with zero hours.
The entry should be displayed correctly.

test_cases.title: Confirm that the system correctly handles empty Work Descriptions.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an active Project from Brac/BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for Hours.
7. Leave the Work Description field blank.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the Work Description is mandatory.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Validate that the system handles large numeric values for Hours correctly.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an active Project from Brac/BracIT Jira.
expected_result: The system should save the entry with the large numeric value for Hours.
The entry should be displayed correctly.

test_cases.title: Verify that the system correctly handles special characters in the Work Description.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an active Project from Brac/BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for Hours.
7. Enter 'This is a test!' for Work Description.
expected_result: The system should save the entry with the Work Description containing special characters.
The entry should be displayed correctly.

test_cases.title: Confirm that the system allows saving the entry with valid data.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date from the current month.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an active Project from Brac/BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for Hours.
expected_result: The system should save the entry with valid data.
The entry should be displayed correctly.

test_cases.title: Verify that the system correctly handles zero hours.
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
expected_result: The entry should be saved successfully with Hours set to 0.
The entry should be displayed in the Timesheet Management module.

test_cases.title: Negative Test - Invalid Project Selection
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select an invalid Project (e.g., non-existent project).
6. Enter a valid numeric value for Hours (e.g., 8).
expected_result: An error message should be displayed indicating that the selected project is invalid.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Boundary Test - Hours at Minimum Value
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
expected_result: The entry should be saved successfully with Hours set to 1.
The entry should be displayed in the Timesheet Management module.

test_cases.title: Boundary Test - Hours at Maximum Value
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
expected_result: The entry should be saved successfully with Hours set to 24.
The entry should be displayed in the Timesheet Management module.

test_cases.title: Negative Test - Empty Description with Valid Hours
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
expected_result: An error message should be displayed indicating that the Description is mandatory.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Positive Test - Valid Data Entry
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
expected_result: The entry should be saved successfully.
The entry should be displayed in the Timesheet Management module.

test_cases.title: Negative Test - Invalid Data Entry - Non-Numeric Hours
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
expected_result: An error message should be displayed indicating that Hours must be a numeric value.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Positive Test - Saving with Existing Data
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
expected_result: The entry should be saved successfully.
The entry should be displayed in the Timesheet Management module.

test_cases.title: Negative Test - Attempting to Save with Empty Fields
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
6. Leave Hours and Description fields blank.
expected_result: Error messages should be displayed indicating that both Hours and Description fields are mandatory.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Positive Test - Saving with Valid Data and Date
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
expected_result: The entry should be saved successfully.
The entry should be displayed in the Timesheet Management module.

test_cases.title: Negative Test - Attempting to Save with Invalid Date
steps: 2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
4. Select a valid Work Log Category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid Project from the Brac/BracIT Jira list.
expected_result: An error message should be displayed indicating that the date is invalid.
The entry should not be saved.
